title: Honor Our Living Donors Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules for a government program that helps pay expenses for people who donate organs to others while they are still alive. Right now, the program only helps donors if the person getting the organ doesn't make too much money, but this bill removes that rule so donors can get help no matter how much the recipient earns. The bill also says donors can get help paying their expenses even if the person getting the organ could afford to pay those costs themselves. The government will have to write a report each year about whether the program has enough money to cover all the expenses that donors need help with.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
subjects: Organ and tissue donation and transplantation

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Honor Our Living Donors Act.

sections.title: Section 2  No Consideration of Income of Organ Recipient
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Public Health Service Act. The changes move around some parts of the law and add a new rule. The new rule says that when organizations get government money to help pay back people who donate organs, they cannot consider how much money the person receiving the organ makes. This means rich and poor organ recipients must be treated the same way when deciding whether to help pay the donor's expenses.

sections.title: Section 3  Removal of Expectation of Payments by Organ Recipients
sections.text: This section removes a requirement from the existing law. Before this change, the law expected that people receiving organs might help pay some costs. This section takes out that expectation completely.

sections.title: Section 4  Annual Report
sections.text: This section requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to write a report every year by December thirty first. The report must be sent to Congress and made available to the public. The report must explain whether the government gave enough money during the past year to pay back all organ donors for their costs. The report must also estimate how many donors did not get paid back for all their costs and how much total money would be needed to pay back all donors completely.
